meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Break the cycle of unfinished projects. Learn a high-speed validation framework using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure your next SaaS earns revenue.
content.answer_block: Stop building in a vacuum. The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of market signal, not a lack of effort. By implementing a rapid 'Validate-First' loop using Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution, you ensure your MVP solves a high-value problem before writing production code.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is paved with perfectly coded features that nobody wanted. You start with a burst of adrenaline, architecting elegant databases and slick UIs, only to hit the 'feature creep' wall three weeks in. That crushing weight of an unfinished project isn't a failure of skill; it is a failure of feedback. 

We are moving from the era of 'build it and they will come' to 'validate it or it dies.' By leveraging AI-assisted scaffolding and rapid execution loops, we can compress months of market research into a weekend of high-intent development, ensuring you only spend your limited time on features that customers are ready to pay for.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Problem
steps.description: Before touching code, document the specific pain point you are solving. Focus on the 'Hair-on-Fire' problem that makes someone reach for their credit card.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Product Architect. Analyze the following pain point: [Insert Problem]. Create a lean 3-feature MVP spec that focuses strictly on resolving this pain point within 48 hours of development time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Draft a 1-page PRD for the MVP identified by Antigravity, focusing on core value delivery only.'

steps.title: Generate Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build the initial directory structure and boilerplate, ensuring you don't waste time on configuration and environment setup.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe boilerplate. Include authentication and a basic billing middleware structure. Keep it modular.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'mkdir my-saas && cd my-saas && antigravity init --stack nextjs-supabase-stripe'

steps.title: Build the Landing Page First
steps.description: Validate demand before writing business logic. Create a landing page that captures emails or pre-orders to prove interest.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting landing page component using Tailwind CSS. Focus on a clear value proposition, social proof placeholders, and a call-to-action that captures user intent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create landing page component with email capture form and integrate with Resend API.'

steps.title: Execute Core Feature Logic
steps.description: Now that you have validation, build the 'One True Feature' that solves the problem. Use Claude Code to maintain clean, maintainable patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for the core feature identified in step 1. Ensure type safety and performant database queries using Prisma.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement the core logic for the [Feature Name] module ensuring database schema alignment with existing tables.'

steps.title: Automated Testing and Deployment
steps.description: Automate your testing and deployment pipeline to ensure your MVP stays stable while you iterate based on user feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an automated GitHub Action workflow that runs unit tests on push and deploys to Vercel upon successful build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'gh workflow create ci-cd-pipeline.yml --template production-ready'

pro_tips: Never build an auth system from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20+ hours immediately.
If you aren't talking to at least 5 potential customers during the build, you are just writing code for your ego.
Use 'Feature Flags' to hide incomplete features so you can deploy to production daily without breaking the user experience.
Prioritize Stripe integration on day one; revenue is the only true metric of product-market fit.
Treat your codebase as ephemeral; if a feature doesn't get traction in 2 weeks, delete the code and pivot.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel, share the link in relevant subreddits or LinkedIn, and track your conversion rate. If you get zero sign-ups, refine your value prop and repeat the cycle.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to stop stalling and start shipping. The only difference between a dreamer and a founder is the willingness to launch imperfectly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
